prose:
Fukui reactivity indices were computed for ferrocene (21 atoms: Fe + C₅H₅ × 2) using the GFN2-xTB semi-empirical tight-binding method (xtb backend) in the gas phase at charge 0, multiplicity 1. The input geometry was taken from the user-supplied ferrocene.xyz file (no relaxation). Nucleophilic (f⁻), electrophilic (f⁺), and dual descriptor (ΔF) indices were calculated via finite-difference Mulliken partial charges derived from N, N+1, and N−1 single-point states. The iron center is the most reactive site: f⁺ = 0.22885456 (electrophilic), f⁻ = 0.25938191 (nucleophilic), dual descriptor ΔF = −0.030527354 (favoring nucleophilic attack). Cyclopentadienyl C atoms show much lower reactivity (~0.0136), and H atoms are intermediate (~0.0635). Mulliken charges reveal the Fe is highly polarized (+0.42 in the neutral species). The calculation converged with full charge conservation (sum(f±) = 1.0000). Integrity status: trustworthy; all internal checks passed.</prose>
<parameter name="value">0.25938191
